actionable_feedback: Add an embedded 'Formula_Audit' sheet in tailings_trigger_review.xlsx that lists key cells (Packet_Input, Piezometer_Review projection & validation cells, Action_Analysis formulas, Decision_Table links) and the exact formula text used, so reviewers can inspect formulas without special libraries.
Alternatively or additionally, include a plaintext export (e.g., [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] or formulas_audit.txt) that maps cell addresses to formula strings for the same key cells.

Explicitly reconcile and clarify the pause-action numeric effect in both the workbook and the memo. State whether 'pause' sets the rise to 0.4x (40% of original) or reduces it by 60% (and update any formulas accordingly).

Add a short scenario-summary table to deposition_decision_memo.md that lists baseline / worse-case / better-case parameter changes (e.g., +-10% rise, freeboard perturbations) and the resulting action label for each scenario so sensitivity outcomes are immediately visible without opening the workbook.

Include a small 'version and validation' block in the workbook and memo (e.g., VERSION, date, brief note confirming projection cells contain formulas not values) so future automated graders can detect formula presence.
Re-run deterministic precheck and attach the plaintext formula export to the revised attempt so graders/verifiers can programmatically validate formulas even when openpyxl is unavailable.
artifact_specific_comments: deposition_decision_memo.md: clear, well-referenced and includes required cells and rationale. Add the scenario summary table and explicit pause-effect statement.

tailings_trigger_review.xlsx: workbook contains expected sheets and high formula density per trace metadata. Add a Formula_Audit sheet or provide a plaintext formula export so graders can inspect formulas without openpyxl.
feedback_summary: Baseline submission satisfies the deliverable contract and scores highly. Two minor issues were identified: (1) programmatic inspection of the .xlsx formulas was not possible in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), leaving a small residual auditability uncertainty; (2) a minor wording ambiguity about the numeric effect of the 'pause' action (trace shows 'pause action=60%' vs memo 'reduces rise to 40%'). Both are minor and do not change the recommended operational decision, but addressing them will improve reproducibility and auditability.

issues.detail: Grader could not programmatically open the .xlsx preview (openpyxl unavailable), leaving residual uncertainty about in-file formula text. Memo and trace support formula presence, but a plaintext formula export would remove ambiguity.
Minor ambiguity about pause action numeric effect: trace 'pause action=60%' vs memo 'reduces rise to 40%'. Clarify whether pause sets rise to 0.4x or reduces it by 60%.

recommended_files_to_add: [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (plaintext mapping of key cells to formula strings)
deposition_decision_memo.md -> add scenario-summary table and explicit pause-effect statement
